string.name: object_info_m42_description
string.translation_description: Description of M42 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Orion Nebula is a diffuse emission nebula visible to the naked eye, one of the most photographed objects in the night sky.

string.name: object_info_m42_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M42 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Orion Nebula is a stellar nursery where new stars are being born right now. Over 700 stars are forming inside!

string.name: object_info_m42_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M42 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m42_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M42 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m45_description
string.translation_description: Description of M45 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pleiades is an open star cluster in Taurus, also known as the Seven Sisters, visible to the naked eye since antiquity.

string.name: object_info_m45_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M45 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pleiades are among the nearest star clusters to Earth and have been recognized by virtually every ancient civilization!

string.name: object_info_m45_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M45 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m45_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M45 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m51_description
string.translation_description: Description of M51 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Whirlpool Galaxy is a grand-design spiral galaxy in Canes Venatici, famous for its striking spiral arms and companion galaxy.

string.name: object_info_m51_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M51 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Whirlpool was the first galaxy to be recognized as having spiral structure, discovered by Lord Rosse in 1845!

string.name: object_info_m51_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M51 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m51_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M51 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m57_description
string.translation_description: Description of M57 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Ring Nebula is a planetary nebula in Lyra, a glowing shell of gas expelled by a dying Sun-like star.

string.name: object_info_m57_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M57 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Ring Nebula is expanding at about 20 km/s and will eventually disperse into space over the next 10,000 years!

string.name: object_info_m57_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M57 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m57_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M57 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m81_description
string.translation_description: Description of M81 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M81 is a grand-design spiral galaxy in Ursa Major, one of the brightest galaxies visible from Earth.

string.name: object_info_m81_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M81 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M81 is gravitationally interacting with neighboring galaxy M82, triggering intense star formation in M82!

string.name: object_info_m81_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M81 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m81_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M81 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m82_description
string.translation_description: Description of M82 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Cigar Galaxy is a starburst galaxy in Ursa Major, known for its edge-on orientation and intense star formation.

string.name: object_info_m82_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M82 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M82 is forming stars 10 times faster than our Milky Way due to gravitational interaction with neighboring M81!

string.name: object_info_m82_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M82 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m82_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M82 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m101_description
string.translation_description: Description of M101 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pinwheel Galaxy is a face-on spiral galaxy in Ursa Major, one of the largest known spiral galaxies.

string.name: object_info_m101_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M101 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pinwheel Galaxy contains over a trillion stars and has asymmetric spiral arms due to past galaxy interactions!

string.name: object_info_m101_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M101 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m101_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M101 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m104_description
string.translation_description: Description of M104 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Sombrero Galaxy is a spiral galaxy in Virgo, famous for its bright nucleus and large central bulge with a dark dust lane.

string.name: object_info_m104_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M104 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Sombrero Galaxy has a supermassive black hole at its center estimated to be 1 billion solar masses!

string.name: object_info_m104_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M104 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m104_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M104 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m8_description
string.translation_description: Description of M8 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Lagoon Nebula is a giant interstellar cloud in Sagittarius, one of the finest and brightest nebulae in the summer sky.

string.name: object_info_m8_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M8 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Lagoon Nebula contains Herschel 36, one of the most massive and luminous stars known!

string.name: object_info_m8_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M8 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m8_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M8 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m17_description
string.translation_description: Description of M17 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Omega Nebula (also Swan Nebula) is a star-forming region in Sagittarius, one of the brightest emission nebulae.

string.name: object_info_m17_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M17 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Omega Nebula contains an open cluster of about 800 young stars hidden within its gas and dust!

string.name: object_info_m17_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M17 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m17_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M17 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m20_description
string.translation_description: Description of M20 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Trifid Nebula is a combination of emission, reflection, and dark nebulae in Sagittarius, divided by dark dust lanes.

string.name: object_info_m20_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M20 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Trifid shows three distinct types of nebulae in one object: red emission, blue reflection, and dark absorption!

string.name: object_info_m20_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M20 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m20_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M20 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m27_description
string.translation_description: Description of M27 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Dumbbell Nebula is a planetary nebula in Vulpecula, the first planetary nebula ever discovered.

string.name: object_info_m27_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M27 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Dumbbell Nebula was discovered by Charles Messier in 1764, making it the first planetary nebula ever discovered!

string.name: object_info_m27_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M27 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m27_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M27 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m33_description
string.translation_description: Description of M33 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Triangulum Galaxy is the third-largest member of the Local Group, a spiral galaxy visible in Triangulum constellation.

string.name: object_info_m33_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M33 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Triangulum Galaxy may be gravitationally bound to the Andromeda Galaxy as a distant satellite!

string.name: object_info_m33_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M33 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m33_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M33 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m35_description
string.translation_description: Description of M35 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M35 is a large open star cluster in Gemini, visible to the naked eye under good conditions.

string.name: object_info_m35_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M35 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M35 contains about 200 stars and appears near a much older cluster NGC 2158 in the same field of view!

string.name: object_info_m35_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M35 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m35_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M35 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m44_description
string.translation_description: Description of M44 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Beehive Cluster (Praesepe) is an open cluster in Cancer, known since ancient times and visible to the naked eye.

string.name: object_info_m44_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M44 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ancient astronomers used the visibility of the Beehive Cluster to predict weather - if invisible, rain was coming!

string.name: object_info_m44_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M44 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m44_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M44 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m78_description
string.translation_description: Description of M78 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M78 is the brightest diffuse reflection nebula in the sky, located in Orion near the famous Belt stars.

string.name: object_info_m78_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M78 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M78 contains about 45 young T Tauri variable stars, very young stars still contracting toward the main sequence!

string.name: object_info_m78_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M78 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m78_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M78 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m83_description
string.translation_description: Description of M83 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Southern Pinwheel is a barred spiral galaxy in Hydra, one of the closest and brightest barred spirals in our sky.

string.name: object_info_m83_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M83 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M83 has produced six observed supernovae, more than any other galaxy - a record for supernova production!

string.name: object_info_m83_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M83 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m83_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M83 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m3_description
string.translation_description: Description of M3 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M3 is one of the largest and brightest globular clusters in the sky, located in Canes Venatici.

string.name: object_info_m3_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M3 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M3 contains about 500,000 stars and is estimated to be 11.4 billion years old!

string.name: object_info_m3_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M3 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m3_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M3 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m5_description
string.translation_description: Description of M5 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M5 is a globular cluster in Serpens, one of the oldest globular clusters at about 13 billion years old.

string.name: object_info_m5_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M5 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M5 contains over 100,000 stars and has one of the highest numbers of known variable stars of any globular cluster!

string.name: object_info_m5_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M5 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m5_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M5 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m15_description
string.translation_description: Description of M15 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M15 is a dense globular cluster in Pegasus, notable for having a planetary nebula at its core - very rare for globular clusters.

string.name: object_info_m15_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M15 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M15 may contain a central black hole of about 4,000 solar masses, though this is still debated!

string.name: object_info_m15_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M15 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m15_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M15 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m22_description
string.translation_description: Description of M22 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M22 is one of the nearest and brightest globular clusters to Earth, located in Sagittarius near the galactic center.

string.name: object_info_m22_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M22 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M22 was one of the first globular clusters discovered, found by Abraham Ihle in 1665!

string.name: object_info_m22_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M22 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m22_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M22 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m4_description
string.translation_description: Description of M4 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M4 is one of the closest globular clusters to Earth and one of the easiest to find, located near Antares in Scorpius.

string.name: object_info_m4_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M4 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M4 contains a white dwarf estimated to be 13 billion years old - one of the oldest known stars in the Milky Way!

string.name: object_info_m4_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M4 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m4_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M4 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m7_description
string.translation_description: Description of M7 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Ptolemy Cluster is a bright open cluster in Scorpius, one of the most prominent clusters visible to the naked eye.

string.name: object_info_m7_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M7 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M7 was first recorded by Ptolemy in 130 AD and has been observed for nearly 2,000 years!

string.name: object_info_m7_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M7 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m7_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M7 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m6_description
string.translation_description: Description of M6 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Butterfly Cluster is an open cluster in Scorpius, named for the butterfly-like arrangement of its brightest stars.

string.name: object_info_m6_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M6 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M6 is about 100 million years old and contains over 300 stars, including the orange giant BM Scorpii!

string.name: object_info_m6_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M6 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m6_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M6 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m11_description
string.translation_description: Description of M11 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Wild Duck Cluster is a rich open cluster in Scutum, one of the most compact open clusters known.

string.name: object_info_m11_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M11 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M11 contains nearly 3,000 stars packed into a small area, creating a spectacular view through telescopes!

string.name: object_info_m11_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M11 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m11_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M11 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m16_description
string.translation_description: Description of M16 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Eagle Nebula is a star-forming nebula in Serpens, famous for the Pillars of Creation imaged by Hubble.

string.name: object_info_m16_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M16 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Pillars of Creation in M16 are about 5 light-years tall and are being slowly eroded by radiation from nearby stars!

string.name: object_info_m16_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M16 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m16_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M16 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m2_description
string.translation_description: Description of M2 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M2 is one of the largest and richest globular clusters known, located in Aquarius.

string.name: object_info_m2_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M2 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M2 contains about 150,000 stars and has a diameter of 175 light-years, making it one of the largest globulars!

string.name: object_info_m2_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M2 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m2_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M2 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m10_description
string.translation_description: Description of M10 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M10 is a bright globular cluster in Ophiuchus, one of the closer globular clusters to our solar system.

string.name: object_info_m10_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M10 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M10 is approaching us at over 70 km/s and has an unusually low concentration of stars at its core!

string.name: object_info_m10_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M10 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m10_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M10 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m12_description
string.translation_description: Description of M12 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M12 is a globular cluster in Ophiuchus, notable for its relatively loose central concentration.

string.name: object_info_m12_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M12 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M12 has lost many of its low-mass stars due to the gravitational pull of the Milky Way over billions of years!

string.name: object_info_m12_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M12 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m12_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M12 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m14_description
string.translation_description: Description of M14 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M14 is a globular cluster in Ophiuchus containing several hundred thousand stars.

string.name: object_info_m14_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M14 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In 1938, a nova appeared in M14 but was not discovered until 1964 when old photographic plates were examined!

string.name: object_info_m14_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M14 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m14_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M14 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m53_description
string.translation_description: Description of M53 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M53 is a globular cluster in Coma Berenices, one of the more outlying globular clusters in our galaxy.

string.name: object_info_m53_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M53 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M53 is one of the most distant globular clusters from the galactic center at 60,000 light-years away from it!

string.name: object_info_m53_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M53 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m53_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M53 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m64_description
string.translation_description: Description of M64 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Black Eye Galaxy is a spiral galaxy in Coma Berenices, famous for the dark band of absorbing dust around its nucleus.

string.name: object_info_m64_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M64 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The outer regions of M64 rotate in the opposite direction from its inner regions, likely from a past galaxy merger!

string.name: object_info_m64_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M64 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m64_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M64 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m65_description
string.translation_description: Description of M65 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M65 is a spiral galaxy in Leo, part of the famous Leo Triplet along with M66 and NGC 3628.

string.name: object_info_m65_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M65 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Leo Triplet galaxies are gravitationally interacting, causing tidal tails and warped structures!

string.name: object_info_m65_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M65 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m65_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M65 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m66_description
string.translation_description: Description of M66 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M66 is the largest member of the Leo Triplet, a spiral galaxy with asymmetric spiral arms.

string.name: object_info_m66_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M66 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M66\'s asymmetric appearance is caused by gravitational interactions with its neighbors M65 and NGC 3628!

string.name: object_info_m66_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M66 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m66_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M66 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m74_description
string.translation_description: Description of M74 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Phantom Galaxy is a face-on spiral galaxy in Pisces, known for its perfect grand-design spiral structure.

string.name: object_info_m74_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M74 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M74 is nicknamed the Phantom Galaxy because of its very low surface brightness, making it challenging to observe!

string.name: object_info_m74_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M74 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m74_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M74 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m77_description
string.translation_description: Description of M77 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M77 is a barred spiral galaxy in Cetus containing an active galactic nucleus powered by a supermassive black hole.

string.name: object_info_m77_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M77 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M77 harbors an active galactic nucleus with a black hole 15 million times the mass of our Sun!

string.name: object_info_m77_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M77 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m77_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M77 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m92_description
string.translation_description: Description of M92 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M92 is a bright globular cluster in Hercules, often overlooked because of its proximity to the more famous M13.

string.name: object_info_m92_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M92 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M92 is one of the oldest globular clusters at about 14.2 billion years old - nearly as old as the universe!

string.name: object_info_m92_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M92 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m92_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M92 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m94_description
string.translation_description: Description of M94 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M94 is a spiral galaxy in Canes Venatici with a bright inner ring where active star formation is occurring.

string.name: object_info_m94_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M94 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M94\'s inner ring is a starburst ring where new stars are forming at a very high rate!

string.name: object_info_m94_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M94 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m94_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M94 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m97_description
string.translation_description: Description of M97 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Owl Nebula is a planetary nebula in Ursa Major, named for its owl-like appearance with two dark spots.

string.name: object_info_m97_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M97 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The dark owl eyes in M97 are three-dimensional cavities in the nebula, aligned with our line of sight!

string.name: object_info_m97_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M97 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m97_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M97 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m106_description
string.translation_description: Description of M106 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M106 is a spiral galaxy in Canes Venatici with anomalous spiral arms caused by jets from its central black hole.

string.name: object_info_m106_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M106 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M106 has water masers in its nucleus, allowing astronomers to precisely measure its distance!

string.name: object_info_m106_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M106 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m106_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M106 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m109_description
string.translation_description: Description of M109 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M109 is a barred spiral galaxy in Ursa Major, the brightest member of a galaxy group bearing its name.

string.name: object_info_m109_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M109 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M109 is located very close to the star Phecda in the Big Dipper, making it easy to locate!

string.name: object_info_m109_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M109 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m109_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M109 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m110_description
string.translation_description: Description of M110 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M110 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy and satellite of the Andromeda Galaxy, the last object added to the Messier catalog.

string.name: object_info_m110_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M110 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M110 was actually observed by Messier in 1773 but not added to his catalog until 1966!

string.name: object_info_m110_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M110 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m110_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M110 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m32_description
string.translation_description: Description of M32 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M32 is a compact dwarf elliptical galaxy and close satellite of the Andromeda Galaxy.

string.name: object_info_m32_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M32 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M32 was the first elliptical galaxy ever discovered and may have once been a larger spiral galaxy stripped by Andromeda!

string.name: object_info_m32_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M32 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m32_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M32 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m49_description
string.translation_description: Description of M49 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M49 is a giant elliptical galaxy in Virgo, the brightest member of the Virgo Cluster.

string.name: object_info_m49_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M49 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M49 was the first member of the Virgo Cluster to be discovered and contains about 6,000 globular clusters!

string.name: object_info_m49_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M49 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m49_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M49 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m87_description
string.translation_description: Description of M87 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M87 is a supergiant elliptical galaxy in Virgo, one of the most massive galaxies in the local universe.

string.name: object_info_m87_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about M87 shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: M87\'s central black hole was the first ever imaged by the Event Horizon Telescope in 2019 - it has 6.5 billion solar masses!

string.name: object_info_m87_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to M87 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m87_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of M87 shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_m87_mass
string.translation_description: Mass of M87 in scientific notation . Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_t_crb_description
string.translation_description: Description of T Crb shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: T Coronae Borealis (T CrB) is a recurrent nova in Corona Borealis — a binary system of a red giant and a white dwarf. The white dwarf periodically accumulates enough hydrogen from its companion to trigger a thermonuclear explosion.

string.name: object_info_t_crb_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about T Crb shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: T CrB last erupted in 1946, briefly reaching magnitude 2.0 and becoming visible to the naked eye. Astronomers predict that another eruption is imminent — keep watching!

string.name: object_info_t_crb_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to T Crb shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_t_crb_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of T Crb shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_tarantula_nebula_description
string.translation_description: Description of the Tarantula Nebula shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Tarantula Nebula is the largest and most luminous star-forming region in the Local Group, a vast emission nebula embedded in the Large Magellanic Cloud spanning some 600 light-years. Despite lying 163,000 light-years away, it is bright enough to be visible to the naked eye.

string.name: object_info_tarantula_nebula_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about the Tarantula Nebula shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: If the Tarantula Nebula were as close as the Orion Nebula, it would be bright enough to cast shadows at night and would span half the visible sky!

string.name: object_info_tarantula_nebula_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to the Tarantula Nebula shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_tarantula_nebula_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of the Tarantula Nebula shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_lmc_description
string.translation_description: Description of the Large Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Large Magellanic Cloud is an irregular satellite galaxy of the Milky Way, visible to the naked eye as a detached, glowing patch of sky. It is one of our closest galactic neighbors and one of the most active star-forming galaxies in the Local Group.

string.name: object_info_lmc_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about the Large Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In 1987, a star in the LMC exploded as Supernova 1987A — the first naked-eye supernova seen since 1604. Neutrinos from the blast reached Earth three hours before the light, confirming our models of stellar core collapse.

string.name: object_info_lmc_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to the Large Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_lmc_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of the Large Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_smc_description
string.translation_description: Description of the Small Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Small Magellanic Cloud is a dwarf irregular satellite galaxy of the Milky Way, paired with its larger sibling the LMC. Together they are slowly being torn apart by the Milky Way\'s gravity, trailing a vast stream of gas across the sky.

string.name: object_info_smc_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about the Small Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: By studying Cepheid variable stars in the SMC in 1908–12, Henrietta Swan Leavitt discovered the period-luminosity law — the breakthrough that first let astronomers measure distances across the universe!

string.name: object_info_smc_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to the Small Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_smc_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of the Small Magellanic Cloud shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_eta_carinae_nebula_description
string.translation_description: Description of Eta Carinae Nebula shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Eta Carinae Nebula (NGC 3372) is one of the largest and brightest nebulae in the sky, spanning over 300 light-years. At its heart sits Eta Carinae, one of the most massive and luminous stars known, weighing over 100 solar masses.

string.name: object_info_eta_carinae_nebula_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Eta Carinae Nebula shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In 1843 Eta Carinae erupted and briefly became the second-brightest star in the sky, yet somehow it survived — it may be on the verge of going supernova!

string.name: object_info_eta_carinae_nebula_distance
string.translation_description: Distance to Eta Carinae Nebula shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_eta_carinae_nebula_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Eta Carinae Nebula shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_orion_description
string.translation_description: Description of Orion shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Orion the Hunter is one of the most recognizable constellations, visible from both hemispheres with its distinctive Belt of three stars.

string.name: object_info_orion_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Orion shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Orion\'s Belt points to Sirius (the brightest star) in one direction and Aldebaran (in Taurus) in the other!

string.name: object_info_orion_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Orion shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_ursa_major_description
string.translation_description: Description of Ursa Major shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ursa Major (the Great Bear) contains the Big Dipper asterism, one of the most recognized star patterns in the northern sky.

string.name: object_info_ursa_major_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Ursa Major shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Five of the Big Dipper stars are physically related, moving through space together as the Ursa Major Moving Group!

string.name: object_info_ursa_major_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Ursa Major shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_ursa_minor_description
string.translation_description: Description of Ursa Minor shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ursa Minor (the Little Bear) contains the Little Dipper and Polaris, the current North Star marking Earth\'s celestial north pole.

string.name: object_info_ursa_minor_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Ursa Minor shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Due to precession, Polaris won\'t always be the North Star - in 14,000 years, Vega will take its place!

string.name: object_info_ursa_minor_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Ursa Minor shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_cassiopeia_description
string.translation_description: Description of Cassiopeia shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cassiopeia is a distinctive W-shaped constellation in the northern sky, named after a vain queen in Greek mythology.

string.name: object_info_cassiopeia_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cassiopeia shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Tycho Brahe\'s supernova of 1572 appeared in Cassiopeia, helping to prove that the heavens could change!

string.name: object_info_cassiopeia_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Cassiopeia shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_scorpius_description
string.translation_description: Description of Scorpius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Scorpius is a prominent zodiac constellation with a distinctive curved tail, dominated by the red supergiant Antares.

string.name: object_info_scorpius_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Scorpius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In mythology, Scorpius was placed in the sky opposite Orion so the hunter would never be stung again!

string.name: object_info_scorpius_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Scorpius shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_leo_description
string.translation_description: Description of Leo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Leo the Lion is a zodiac constellation featuring Regulus at the heart of the lion and the distinctive Sickle asterism.

string.name: object_info_leo_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Leo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Leonid meteor shower in November appears to radiate from Leo and can produce spectacular storms!

string.name: object_info_leo_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Leo shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_gemini_description
string.translation_description: Description of Gemini shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Gemini the Twins features the bright stars Castor and Pollux, representing the twin brothers from Greek mythology.

string.name: object_info_gemini_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Gemini shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Despite being called twins, Castor is actually six stars and Pollux is an orange giant - they look nothing alike!

string.name: object_info_gemini_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Gemini shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_taurus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Taurus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Taurus the Bull is a zodiac constellation containing the Pleiades and Hyades star clusters, and the bright star Aldebaran.

string.name: object_info_taurus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Taurus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Crab Nebula in Taurus is the remnant of a supernova witnessed and recorded by Chinese astronomers in 1054 AD!

string.name: object_info_taurus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Taurus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_virgo_description
string.translation_description: Description of Virgo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Virgo is the second largest constellation and a zodiac sign, containing the Virgo Cluster of over 2,000 galaxies.

string.name: object_info_virgo_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Virgo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Virgo Cluster is the heart of the Virgo Supercluster, which contains our own Milky Way galaxy!

string.name: object_info_virgo_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Virgo shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_aquarius_description
string.translation_description: Description of Aquarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Aquarius the Water Bearer is a zodiac constellation in a region of sky often called the Sea, surrounded by water-related constellations.

string.name: object_info_aquarius_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Aquarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Aquarius contains the Helix Nebula, one of the closest planetary nebulae to Earth at only 700 light-years away!

string.name: object_info_aquarius_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Aquarius shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_pisces_description
string.translation_description: Description of Pisces shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Pisces the Fishes is a large zodiac constellation representing two fish tied together, marking the vernal equinox point.

string.name: object_info_pisces_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Pisces shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The vernal equinox (first day of spring) currently lies in Pisces and has been slowly moving through it for 2,000 years!

string.name: object_info_pisces_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Pisces shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_aries_description
string.translation_description: Description of Aries shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Aries the Ram is a zodiac constellation that was once home to the vernal equinox, giving us the phrase \"First Point of Aries.\"

string.name: object_info_aries_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Aries shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Though the vernal equinox moved to Pisces about 2,000 years ago, we still call it the First Point of Aries!

string.name: object_info_aries_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Aries shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_cancer_description
string.translation_description: Description of Cancer shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cancer the Crab is the faintest zodiac constellation but contains the famous Beehive Cluster (M44) visible to the naked eye.

string.name: object_info_cancer_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cancer shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Tropic of Cancer was named when the Sun was in Cancer at the summer solstice, though it\'s now in Taurus!

string.name: object_info_cancer_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Cancer shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_capricornus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Capricornus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Capricornus the Sea-Goat is an ancient zodiac constellation depicting a mythical creature that\'s half goat, half fish.

string.name: object_info_capricornus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Capricornus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Capricornus is one of the oldest recognized constellations, appearing in Babylonian star catalogs over 3,000 years ago!

string.name: object_info_capricornus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Capricornus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_sagittarius_description
string.translation_description: Description of Sagittarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Sagittarius the Archer points toward the center of our Milky Way galaxy, making it rich in nebulae and star clusters.

string.name: object_info_sagittarius_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Sagittarius shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: When you look at Sagittarius, you\'re looking toward Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at our galaxy\'s center!

string.name: object_info_sagittarius_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Sagittarius shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_libra_description
string.translation_description: Description of Libra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Libra the Scales is the only zodiac constellation representing an inanimate object, once considered part of Scorpius.

string.name: object_info_libra_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Libra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Libra\'s main stars were once known as the Claws of the Scorpion before becoming the Scales of Justice!

string.name: object_info_libra_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Libra shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_cygnus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Cygnus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cygnus the Swan flies along the Milky Way, containing the Northern Cross asterism and the bright star Deneb.

string.name: object_info_cygnus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cygnus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cygnus X-1 in this constellation was the first widely accepted black hole, identified in the early 1970s!

string.name: object_info_cygnus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Cygnus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_lyra_description
string.translation_description: Description of Lyra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Lyra the Lyre is a small but prominent constellation containing Vega, the fifth brightest star in the night sky.

string.name: object_info_lyra_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Lyra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Vega was the North Star around 12,000 BC and will be again in about 12,000 years due to Earth\'s precession!

string.name: object_info_lyra_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Lyra shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_aquila_description
string.translation_description: Description of Aquila shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Aquila the Eagle is a constellation on the celestial equator, containing the bright star Altair at its center.

string.name: object_info_aquila_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Aquila shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Altair in Aquila is one of the fastest rotating stars known, spinning so fast it bulges at its equator!

string.name: object_info_aquila_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Aquila shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_pegasus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Pegasus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Pegasus the Winged Horse is a large constellation featuring the Great Square of Pegasus, a prominent autumn asterism.

string.name: object_info_pegasus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Pegasus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: 51 Pegasi b, discovered in 1995 in this constellation, was the first exoplanet found orbiting a Sun-like star!

string.name: object_info_pegasus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Pegasus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_andromeda_description
string.translation_description: Description of Andromeda shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Andromeda the Princess contains the Andromeda Galaxy (M31), the nearest large spiral galaxy to our Milky Way.

string.name: object_info_andromeda_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Andromeda shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Andromeda Galaxy is approaching us at 110 km/s and will merge with the Milky Way in about 4.5 billion years!

string.name: object_info_andromeda_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Andromeda shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_perseus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Perseus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Perseus the Hero is home to the famous variable star Algol and the Double Cluster, a stunning pair of open clusters.

string.name: object_info_perseus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Perseus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Algol (the Demon Star) was considered evil in ancient times because it mysteriously dims every 2.87 days!

string.name: object_info_perseus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Perseus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_centaurus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Centaurus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Centaurus the Centaur is a large southern constellation containing Alpha Centauri, the nearest star system to our Sun.

string.name: object_info_centaurus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Centaurus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Proxima Centauri in Centaurus is only 4.24 light-years away, making it the closest star to our solar system!

string.name: object_info_centaurus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Centaurus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_crux_description
string.translation_description: Description of Crux shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Crux (the Southern Cross) is the smallest constellation but one of the most distinctive, used for navigation in the Southern Hemisphere.

string.name: object_info_crux_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Crux shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Despite being the smallest constellation, Crux has more bright stars per unit area than any other constellation!

string.name: object_info_crux_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Crux shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_canis_major_description
string.translation_description: Description of Canis Major shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Canis Major (the Great Dog) follows Orion across the sky and contains Sirius, the brightest star visible from Earth.

string.name: object_info_canis_major_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Canis Major shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ancient Egyptians based their calendar on the heliacal rising of Sirius, which coincided with the Nile\'s annual flood!

string.name: object_info_canis_major_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Canis Major shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_canis_minor_description
string.translation_description: Description of Canis Minor shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Canis Minor (the Lesser Dog) is a small constellation notable for Procyon, the eighth brightest star in the sky.

string.name: object_info_canis_minor_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Canis Minor shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Procyon means \"before the dog\" in Greek because it rises just before Sirius (the Dog Star) each night!

string.name: object_info_canis_minor_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Canis Minor shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_cepheus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Cepheus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cepheus the King is a northern constellation whose stars include Delta Cephei, the prototype for Cepheid variable stars.

string.name: object_info_cepheus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cepheus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cepheid variables discovered by Henrietta Leavitt revolutionized astronomy by allowing us to measure cosmic distances!

string.name: object_info_cepheus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Cepheus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_draco_description
string.translation_description: Description of Draco shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Draco the Dragon is a long, winding constellation that circles around Ursa Minor in the far northern sky.

string.name: object_info_draco_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Draco shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Thuban in Draco was the North Star when the Egyptian pyramids were built, 5,000 years ago!

string.name: object_info_draco_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Draco shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_hercules_description
string.translation_description: Description of Hercules shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Hercules is a large constellation representing the Greek hero, containing the great globular cluster M13.

string.name: object_info_hercules_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Hercules shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Our solar system is moving toward a point in Hercules called the solar apex at about 20 km/s!

string.name: object_info_hercules_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Hercules shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_ophiuchus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Ophiuchus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Ophiuchus the Serpent Bearer lies along the ecliptic but isn\'t a zodiac sign, divided by the serpent he holds.

string.name: object_info_ophiuchus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Ophiuchus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Sun spends more time in Ophiuchus than Scorpius, yet Ophiuchus isn\'t considered a zodiac sign!

string.name: object_info_ophiuchus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Ophiuchus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_bootes_description
string.translation_description: Description of Bootes shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Bootes the Herdsman is a constellation containing Arcturus, the fourth brightest star in the night sky.

string.name: object_info_bootes_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Bootes shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Follow the arc of the Big Dipper\'s handle to \"arc to Arcturus\" - one of the most famous navigation tips!

string.name: object_info_bootes_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Bootes shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_corona_borealis_description
string.translation_description: Description of Corona Borealis shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Corona Borealis (the Northern Crown) is a small semicircular constellation representing Ariadne\'s crown from Greek mythology.

string.name: object_info_corona_borealis_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Corona Borealis shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: T Coronae Borealis is a recurrent nova that erupts roughly every 80 years - another eruption may happen soon!

string.name: object_info_corona_borealis_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Corona Borealis shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_corvus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Corvus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Corvus the Crow is a small quadrilateral constellation south of Virgo, representing Apollo\'s sacred bird.

string.name: object_info_corvus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Corvus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The four main stars of Corvus form a distinctive sail shape that\'s easy to spot in the spring sky!

string.name: object_info_corvus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Corvus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_auriga_description
string.translation_description: Description of Auriga shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Auriga the Charioteer is a northern constellation featuring Capella, the sixth brightest star, and several open clusters.

string.name: object_info_auriga_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Auriga shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Epsilon Aurigae is one of the strangest stars known, dimming for two years every 27 years due to a mysterious dark disk!

string.name: object_info_auriga_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Auriga shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_eridanus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Eridanus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Eridanus the River is the sixth largest constellation, winding from near Orion to the far southern sky.

string.name: object_info_eridanus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Eridanus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Achernar at the end of Eridanus is the flattest star known, bulging 56% wider at its equator due to rapid rotation!

string.name: object_info_eridanus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Eridanus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_lepus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Lepus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Lepus the Hare crouches beneath Orion\'s feet, a small but distinctive constellation with a rich mythology.

string.name: object_info_lepus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Lepus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: In mythology, Orion was said to love hunting hares, so one was placed beneath his feet in the sky!

string.name: object_info_lepus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Lepus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_monoceros_description
string.translation_description: Description of Monoceros shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Monoceros the Unicorn is a faint constellation in the winter Milky Way, rich in nebulae and star clusters.

string.name: object_info_monoceros_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Monoceros shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Rosette Nebula in Monoceros is a massive star-forming region resembling a celestial rose!

string.name: object_info_monoceros_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Monoceros shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_hydra_description
string.translation_description: Description of Hydra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Hydra is the largest constellation in the sky, representing the water serpent from Greek mythology.

string.name: object_info_hydra_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Hydra shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Hydra stretches over 100 degrees of sky - more than a quarter of the way around the entire celestial sphere!

string.name: object_info_hydra_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Hydra shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_carina_description
string.translation_description: Description of Carina shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Carina the Keel was part of the huge Argo Navis constellation, containing the brilliant star Canopus.

string.name: object_info_carina_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Carina shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Eta Carinae in Carina is one of the most luminous stars known and may explode as a supernova in our lifetime!

string.name: object_info_carina_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Carina shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_vela_description
string.translation_description: Description of Vela shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Vela the Sails was once part of the great ship Argo Navis, located in a rich region of the Milky Way.

string.name: object_info_vela_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Vela shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Vela Pulsar is the brightest pulsar in the sky and the remnant of a supernova that exploded 11,000 years ago!

string.name: object_info_vela_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Vela shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_puppis_description
string.translation_description: Description of Puppis shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Puppis the Stern represents the rear deck of the ship Argo Navis, rich in open clusters along the Milky Way.

string.name: object_info_puppis_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Puppis shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Puppis contains M47, an open cluster visible to the naked eye that was \"lost\" for over 200 years due to a cataloging error!

string.name: object_info_puppis_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Puppis shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_cetus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Cetus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Cetus the Sea Monster is a large constellation containing Mira, the first variable star ever discovered.

string.name: object_info_cetus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Cetus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Mira in Cetus varies from visible to invisible over 11 months and has a 13-light-year-long tail of gas!

string.name: object_info_cetus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Cetus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_phoenix_description
string.translation_description: Description of Phoenix shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Phoenix is a southern constellation representing the mythical bird that rises from its own ashes.

string.name: object_info_phoenix_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Phoenix shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Phoenix was one of 12 constellations created by Dutch explorers in the late 16th century from uncharted southern stars!

string.name: object_info_phoenix_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Phoenix shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_grus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Grus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Grus the Crane is a southern constellation introduced in the late 16th century by European navigators.

string.name: object_info_grus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Grus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Grus was once known as Phoenicopterus (the Flamingo) before being changed to represent a crane!

string.name: object_info_grus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Grus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_pavo_description
string.translation_description: Description of Pavo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Pavo the Peacock is a southern constellation containing the bright star Peacock and several interesting galaxies.

string.name: object_info_pavo_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Pavo shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The star Peacock (Alpha Pavonis) is the second most luminous star within 200 light-years of our Sun!

string.name: object_info_pavo_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Pavo shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_triangulum_description
string.translation_description: Description of Triangulum shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Triangulum is a small northern constellation containing the Triangulum Galaxy (M33), the third largest in the Local Group.

string.name: object_info_triangulum_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Triangulum shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: The Triangulum Galaxy is the most distant object that can be seen with the naked eye under ideal conditions!

string.name: object_info_triangulum_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Triangulum shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_delphinus_description
string.translation_description: Description of Delphinus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Delphinus the Dolphin is a small but distinctive constellation whose stars form the shape of a leaping dolphin.

string.name: object_info_delphinus_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Delphinus shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Two of Delphinus\'s stars are named Sualocin and Rotanev - \"Nicolaus Venator\" spelled backwards, the astronomer who named them!

string.name: object_info_delphinus_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Delphinus shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.

string.name: object_info_sagitta_description
string.translation_description: Description of Sagitta shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Sagitta the Arrow is the third smallest constellation, representing various mythological arrows from Greek legends.

string.name: object_info_sagitta_funfact
string.translation_description: Fun fact about Sagitta shown in the info card. Translate this text.

resources.string: Despite being tiny, Sagitta contains M71, a globular cluster that was mistaken for an open cluster for centuries!

string.name: object_info_sagitta_size
string.translation_description: Physical size of Sagitta shown in the info card. Translate units if appropriate for your locale.
